description: Cost-optimization epic, candidate #3 (docs/prd/cost-optimization-candidates.md §2, owner-contributed; made more aggressive per owner direction 2026-07-07). A MULTI-AGENT decomposition of the daily brief that pushes Haiku as far as it will go while keeping Sonnet ONLY where editorial judgment lives. A Sonnet coordinator orchestrates four sub-agents, each of which invokes the SAME unchanged daily-ai-brief skill (skill_01H2qu83NwnJ5zqcbrqsCcJ6 @ 1783340601977967) scoped to a slice of that skill's own numbered Daily workflow -- so the skill prose is byte-identical to production-baseline and this candidate isolates STRUCTURE + per-phase MODEL, not prompt wording. Roster: research sub-agent (HAIKU, skill steps 1-3: set up, gather, paywall); selection sub-agent (SONNET, skill steps 4-5: dedupe & cluster, rank & select, + candidates.json); writing sub-agent (HAIKU, skill steps 6-7: write, finish, + the brief + source-usage.json); listening-script sub-agent (HAIKU, the skill's 'audio / listening-script output' section). Sonnet is retained ONLY for coordination and editorial selection -- the two phases where source/dedup judgment and hallucination risk concentrate; Haiku does the high-volume gathering, the token-heavy writing (the biggest cost phase per the original analysis, ~4.2M vs ~1.2M cache-read tokens), and the mechanical listening-script rewrite. The factual-accuracy judge is the make-or-break metric here (Haiku on research concentrates fabrication risk; Sonnet-selection is the safety net that vets what Haiku gathered). Content generation ONLY: holds no AWS/delivery role (FR-1), never fans out to subscribers, never synthesizes audio (no TTS in evals, owner-confirmed 2026-07-06); its only delivery-side contact is the read-only GET /recent-briefs route. The coordinator and each sub-agent are SEPARATE, newly-created agent resources (their own agent_ids, written once at first sync). A-VERIFICATION ITEM: the exact multi-agent execution semantics -- how the coordinator delegates and whether sub-agents share the /workspace working folder for artifact passing -- is verified in step A (eval-harness re-integration) and may refine the sub-agent delegation wording, but not the models/structure/skill-scoping declared here.
schedule_intent: none -- cost-optimization eval candidate only; never scheduled and never promoted to production without a separate, explicitly-approved cut-over decision
